title: Amang The Trees.
content:
Tune - "The King of France, he rade a race."



I.

        Amang the trees, where humming bees
            At buds and flowers were hinging, O,
        Auld Caledon drew out her drone,
            And to her pipe was singing, O;
        'Twas pibroch, sang, strathspey, or reels,
            She dirl'd them aff fu' clearly, O,
        When there cam a yell o' foreign squeels,
            That dang her tapsalteerie, O.

II.

        Their capon craws and queer ha ha's,
            They made our lugs grow eerie, O;
        The hungry bike did scrape and pike,
            'Till we were wae and weary, O;
        But a royal ghaist wha ance was cas'd
            A prisoner aughteen year awa,
        He fir'd a fiddler in the north
            That dang them tapsalteerie, O.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "Amang The Trees.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
